title: gelseminum speciosum
intro: Gelseminum speciosum, also known as Yellow Jessamine, is a poisonous plant native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and woody vines. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. However, some studies suggest that the plant may have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating pain, but its use is often cautioned due to potential toxicity. In herbal preparations, Gelseminum speciosum is typically used in small doses, often combined with other herbs, and should be administered under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
